key_insight: HiReLC uses a two-level reinforcement learning controller to jointly prune and quantize neural networks while allocating compression budget using sensitivity-aware global coordination.
engineering_relevance: This provides an architecture-agnostic automation strategy for reducing model size and compute without manually tuning compression settings for each layer or network.
method: Low-level agents choose per-block settings for bitwidth, pruning ratio, quantization type, and granularity.
High-level agents vote on global budget allocation using Fisher Information-based sensitivity estimates.
An active-learning loop uses a lightweight surrogate and post-compression fine-tuning to reduce expensive evaluations.
evidence_signal: Across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks, the method achieved about 5.99–6.72× parameter-storage compression with accuracy changes ranging from a 3.83% gain to 0.55–5.62% drops.
watch_item: Because reward shaping relies on a surrogate model, performance may depend on surrogate quality and careful calibration during cold-start and fine-tuning.
paper_title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
